preamble: The transformation of the pharyngeal 'ayin' into the open-mid front vowel 'epsilon' (ε) is a specific outcome of vowel coloring or vocalization. This can be seen as a lower-mid realization of the 'e-coloring' effect observed in Akkadian and some modern Semitic dialects.

examples.note: Common in Modern Israeli speakers where pharyngeal distinction is lost.
Though Ayin typically became Omicron, the general process of turning gutturals into front vowels like epsilon is well-documented.

sources: Woodard, R. D. (1997). Greek Writing from Knossos to Homer.
